text: Last class you chaired a meeting and made suggestions.
Today you learn to agree and disagree in a diplomatic way.
Point of view
Agree with
Disagree
Softener
Push back
Concede
Valid
On the same page
See your point
Fair enough
Compromise
Raise a concern
I see your point, but I have to push back.
That is valid, but I am not sure about the cost.
I agree with the price, however the quality worries me.
Fair enough, but let us find a compromise.
From my point of view, the new supplier is a valid option. The price is lower.
I agree with you on the price. But I see your point differently on quality.
I have to push back a little, because I want to raise a concern about delivery.
Fair enough. I concede that delivery is important. We are not on the same page yet.
So let us find a compromise. We could test the supplier with a small order first.
Now we are on the same page. I agree with that plan.
I see your point, but I have to push back a little.
That is a valid concern, and I agree with part of it.
Fair enough, but I am not sure we are on the same page.
Let me share my point of view before we decide.
Let us find a compromise that works for both teams.

Hi Walyson, this is Sofia. Before the debate tomorrow, I want to share my point of view on the new supplier. I agree with the price, and I think it is a valid option for the next quarter. However, I have to push back a little on the delivery time, because it worries me. I see your point that the supplier is cheaper, but quality matters more to me. Maybe we are not on the same page yet, and that is fine. I am sure we can find a compromise. Fair enough if you disagree, but please come ready to defend your idea with clear reasons. See you tomorrow.

This is my summary of our debate about the new coffee supplier. At first, Sofia and I were not on the same page. I agreed with her on the price, but I had to push back on the quality. She raised a concern about the delivery time, which was a valid point. I told her, I see your point, and fair enough, but speed is important for our clients. In the end, we did not fully agree, so we reached a compromise. We will test the supplier with a small order first. I think it was a fair decision, and we both feel comfortable with it.

First, listen to the other person and show you understand their point of view. Next, agree with the part that is valid. After that, use a softener before you push back. Then, give a clear reason for your own opinion. Finally, suggest a compromise so you can move forward together.
